title: 10 best herbal tinctures for hypothyroidism
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for hypothyroidism are concentrated liquid extracts of plant-based herbs that can help alleviate symptoms of an underactive thyroid gland. These tinctures work by stimulating the production of thyroid hormones, improving metabolism and energy levels, and reducing fatigue. Examples of herbal tinctures that have shown benefits in managing hypothyroidism include Ashwagandha, Bladderwrack, and Thyroidinum. By taking these tinctures, individuals with hypothyroidism may experience improved mood, increased energy, and enhanced overall well-being, allowing them to live more active and fulfilling lives.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Milk thistle tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they contain silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that supports liver function. The liver plays a crucial role in regulating thyroid hormone production, and milk thistle's ability to protect and detoxify the liver can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, dry skin, and weight gain. Additionally, milk thistle tinctures have been shown to improve thyroid function by increasing the production of T4 and T3 hormones, promoting overall health and well-being in individuals with hypothyroidism.
remedy_properties: Silymarin: Silymarin, a flavonoid compound found in milk thistle, has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals and inflammation, which is common in hypothyroidism.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids present in milk thistle tinctures have been shown to improve thyroid hormone production and metabolism by increasing the activity of enzymes involved in thyroid hormone synthesis and reducing the activity of enzymes that inhibit thyroid hormone production.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, such as silibinin and isosilibinin, found in milk thistle tinctures, have been shown to have thyroprotective effects by inhibiting the uptake of radioactive iodine (a common treatment for hyperthyroidism) and reducing the risk of thyroid damage.
remedy_parts: Roots: Milk thistle roots have been traditionally used to treat hypothyroidism due to their high concentration of silymarin, a powerful antioxidant that helps protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and inflammation.

Seeds: Milk thistle seeds contain a unique compound called silibinin, which has been shown to inhibit the production of antibodies involved in autoimmune thyroiditis, a common cause of hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality silybum marianum dried flowers and leaves in the morning after dew has evaporated weighing approximately 100 grams.
Clean and chop the plant material into small pieces using scissors or a mortar and pestle to ensure maximum extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 700 milliliters of 95 percent ethanol in a clean glass jar ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place allowing the tincture to mature and infuse with medicinal properties.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding solids and storing the liquid tincture in a cool dark place.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ashwagandha tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it supports thyroid function by reducing cortisol levels, which can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin. The herb's adaptogenic properties also promote a sense of balance and calm, helping to regulate the body's metabolic rate. Additionally, ashwagandha has been shown to increase thyroid hormone production, potentially improving energy levels and overall well-being. By addressing underlying stress and hormonal imbalances, ashwagandha tinctures can help alleviate hypothyroidism symptoms and promote a healthier thyroid function.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Ashwagandha helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which can alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and lethargy by supporting thyroid function.
Antioxidant property: Ashwagandha's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and reduce its functioning, thus promoting overall thyroid health.

Thyroid-stimulating property: Ashwagandha has been shown to stimulate the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3), two essential hormones produced by the thyroid gland that regulate metabolism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ashwagandha plant are used to make medicinal tinctures for hypothyroidism because they contain a rich concentration of withanolides, which have been shown to stimulate thyroid function and regulate metabolism.

Barks: Ashwagandha barks are used to make medicinal tinctures for hypothyroidism because they contain bioactive compounds like withaferin A, which has been shown to increase T3 levels and improve thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried roots of withania somnifera in the morning when dew is still present on leaves for optimal bioavailability.
Grind the dried roots into fine powder using mortar and pestle to ensure uniform particle size for efficient extraction.
Combine one part powdered root with three parts high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a glass jar filled to the brim for maceration.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily, to allow for thorough solvent extraction of active compounds.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth and discard the solids before transferring the tincture to amber glass bottles with tight-fitting lids for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Guggulu tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they contain compounds that stimulate the thyroid gland, promoting increased production of thyroid hormones. The resin of Commiphora mukul, used to make guggulu tincture, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to improve thyroid function. Guggulu's active components, such as guggulsterones and guggulonic acid, help to increase the uptake of iodine by the thyroid gland, allowing for more effective production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3). This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, including fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin.
remedy_properties: Thyroid Stimulating Properties: Guggulu's ability to stimulate thyroid function helps to increase production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3), which are essential for maintaining normal metabolism in individuals with hypothyroidism.

Anti-Inflammatory Effects: Guggulu's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, which is often present in cases of hypothyroidism, and alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, joint pain, and stiffness.

Adaptogenic Properties: Guggulu's adaptogenic properties allow it to help the body adapt to stress and hormonal imbalances, which is particularly beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who may experience fatigue, mood swings, and other symptoms related to stress and hormonal imbalance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the guggulu plant are used to make tinctures as they contain a high concentration of guggulsterones, which have been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve thyroid function in individuals with hypothyroidism.

Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) of the guggulu plant are used due to their rich content of sesquiterpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and joint pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried commiphora wightii resin from the plant's bark, stems, and leaves in late autumn or winter.
Prepare the herb by chopping it into small pieces and mixing with 80% vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Fill the jar to the top with the herb mixture and add 20-30 drops of grapefruit seed extract as a preservative.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it twice daily to ensure extraction of the herb's active compounds.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the final product in an amber-colored glass bottle in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Indian tobacco tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the thyroid gland, enhancing its function and increasing the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3). The tannins and flavonoids present in Indian tobacco also improve circulation, which aids in the distribution of hormones to various parts of the body. Additionally, Indian tobacco's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress, common contributors to hypothyroidism.
remedy_properties: Respiratory stimulant: Lobelia's ability to stimulate the respiratory system can help improve thyroid function by increasing oxygenation and circulation, which is essential for proper thyroid hormone production.

Adaptogenic and stress-relieving properties: Indian tobacco's adaptogenic effects can help reduce stress and anxiety, which are common underlying factors in hypothyroidism. By reducing cortisol levels and promoting relaxation, Lobelia may help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms.

Thyroid-supportive compounds: Lobelia contains compounds like lobeline and lobelanine, which have been shown to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve its function, potentially helping to regulate hormone production and alleviate hypothyroidism symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Lobelia inflata have been traditionally used to stimulate the thyroid gland and improve overall thyroid function.
Barks: The barks of Lobelia inflata contain compounds that have been shown to help regulate thyroid hormones and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh lobelia inflata leaves at dawn or early morning for maximum potency and store them in airtight containers.
Chop the harvested lobelia inflata leaves into small pieces to ensure complete extraction of their active compounds during maceration.
Mix 1 part chopped lobelia inflata leaves with 2 parts 80% ethanol in a clean glass jar, ensuring all plant material is fully submerged in the solvent.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction of bioactive compounds from the lobelia inflata leaves.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the plant material and storing the resulting liquid at room temperature until use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it has a harmonizing effect on the body's energy, or "qi," which can help regulate thyroid function. The herb is believed to warm and nourish the kidneys, which are associated with thyroid health in traditional Chinese medicine. Additionally, dong quai's adaptogenic properties allow it to support the body's response to stress, which is often a contributing factor to hypothyroidism. By addressing these underlying factors, dong quai tinctures may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic and energizing properties: Dong quai helps to boost energy levels and adapt to stress, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals with hypothyroidism who often experience fatigue.

Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects: The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds in dong quai may help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction and improve overall health.

Tonic properties: Dong quai is known for its tonifying effects on the body's energy (qi) and blood, which can help to balance and regulate thyroid function, as well as alleviate symptoms such as weight gain, dry skin, and hair loss that are common in hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and flavonoids, Dong Quai roots have been traditionally used to tonify the kidneys and liver, which can help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzome is a key component of Dong Quai tinctures for hypothyroidism as it contains bioactive compounds that promote hormonal balance, including estrogen-like activities.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh angelica sinensis roots and leaves following organic farming practices.
Clean and dry the harvested materials by gently brushing off debris and excess moisture.
Chop the clean angelica sinensis into small pieces and combine with 70% ethanol at a ratio of 10:1.
Steep the mixture for 2-4 weeks in a warm dark place, shaking the container daily for optimal extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and store it in dark glass bottles with tight-fitting lids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maca tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they support the thyroid gland's natural function, stimulating production of hormones that regulate metabolism. Rich in nutrients like iron, calcium, and vitamin C, maca root extract enhances thyroid function, alleviating symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and cold intolerance. Additionally, maca tinctures have been shown to improve energy levels, mood, and overall well-being by balancing the body's hormone production, making it a natural and effective adjunct therapy for hypothyroidism sufferers.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic effects: Maca tincture helps to regulate hormonal balance, including thyroid hormones, by adapting to the body's needs and reducing stress levels.
Antioxidant activity: Maca tincture's high antioxidant content helps protect the thyroid gland from oxidative damage and inflammation, which can contribute to hypothyroidism.

Immunomodulatory effects: Maca tincture modulates the immune system, which may help to reduce autoimmune responses that can lead to hypothyroidism by decreasing inflammation and promoting a healthy balance of white blood cells.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, maca roots are considered a powerful adaptogen that helps regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Rhyzomes: As the underground stems of the plant, rhyzomes contain unique bioflavonoids that have been shown to stimulate thyroid hormone production and improve overall thyroid health.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried lepidium meyenii roots or seeds in sufficient quantity for the desired dosage and preparation method.
Grind the dried herb into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or food processor to facilitate extraction.
Combine 10 grams of the ground herbal material with 100 milliliters of high-proof vodka or rum in a clean glass jar, ensuring proper ratio for tincture making.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for at least four weeks to allow for slow and efficient extraction of bioactive compounds from the herb.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discard solids and store the final tincture in a dark glass bottle with tight-fitting lid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Saw palmetto tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it has been shown to support thyroid function by blocking the conversion of testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), a hormone that can contribute to hypothyroid symptoms. By reducing DHT levels, saw palmetto may help increase thyroxine (T4) production and improve overall thyroid function. Additionally, its antioxidant properties may also help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, further supporting its potential benefits for individuals with hypothyroidism.
remedy_properties: Serenoide content: Saw palmetto's serenoide content helps to increase thyroid hormone production by inhibiting the conversion of T4 to RT3, a less active form of thyroid hormone. This allows for increased levels of T4 and T3 in the bloodstream.

5-alpha-reductase inhibition: Saw palmetto's ability to inhibit 5-alpha-reductase, an enzyme that converts testosterone to dihydrotestosterone (DHT), may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism by reducing inflammation and improving thyroid function.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Saw palmetto's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation associated with hypothyroidism, which can contribute to fatigue, brain fog, and other symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in fatty acids and phytosterols, which help regulate thyroid hormone production and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Fruits: The fruits contain a unique combination of fatty acids, particularly oleic acid, which helps to support the body's natural thyroid function and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 cups of fresh sideroxylon lanuginosum leaves or 1 cup dried material.
Combine the herbs with 4 cups of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a dark place for 4-6 weeks shaking daily to allow extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth and discard solids after 24 hours.
Transfer the liquid to amber glass bottles and store in the refrigerator for up to.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginseng tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because of its ability to stimulate thyroid function. The plant-based compounds in ginseng, such as ginsenosides and saponins, have been shown to increase the production of thyroid hormones, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with hypothyroidism. Additionally, ginseng's adaptogenic properties help the body adapt to stress, which is often a contributing factor to thyroid dysfunction. By supporting thyroid function and reducing stress, herbal ginseng tinctures can provide relief from fatigue, weight gain, and other symptoms commonly experienced by individuals with hypothyroidism.
remedy_properties: Adaptogenic property: Ginseng tincture helps to adapt the body's response to stress, which is often exacerbated by hypothyroidism, by normalizing cortisol levels and reducing oxidative stress.
Antioxidant activity: Ginseng tincture's antioxidant properties help to combat free radicals that can damage thyroid tissue and disrupt thyroid hormone production, thereby supporting thyroid health.
Immunomodulatory effect: Ginseng tincture modulates the immune system, which can help to regulate autoimmune responses that may contribute to hypothyroidism, such as Hashimoto's thyroiditis.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in bioactive compounds that have been shown to stimulate thyroid function and improve symptoms associated with hypothyroidism.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes contain saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of fatigue and lethargy common in hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried panax ginseng roots in the fall season or purchase from reputable suppliers at least one pound of high-quality herb.
Clean and dry the panax ginseng roots by gently brushing off any dirt or debris and storing them in an airtight container for two days.
Chop the cleaned and dried panax ginseng roots into small pieces to increase their surface area for extraction using scissors or a food processor.
Combine one pound of chopped panax ginseng roots with 100 proof vodka in a clean glass jar covering the herb completely and storing it in a cool dark place for four weeks.

Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles using 10 drops per dose three times daily for optimal results.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been shown to increase the production of thyroid hormones. Glycyrrhizin also helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. Additionally, licorice root has been found to improve adrenal function, which is often compromised in individuals with hypothyroidism. By addressing these underlying issues, licorice tinctures may help to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and weight gain.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, promoting healthy thyroid function.

Flavonoids: Licorice flavonoids have been shown to have thyroprotective effects by reducing the production of thyroid antibodies and preventing damage to thyroid cells, thus supporting overall thyroid health.

Glycyrretin: This compound has been found to stimulate the production of T4 (thyroxine) and T3 (triiodothyronine), two essential hormones produced by the thyroid gland that regulate metabolism and energy levels.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue and joint pain.
Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes, or underground stems, contain a higher concentration of glycosides, which have been shown to have immunomodulatory effects and may help regulate thyroid function.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried licorice roots of glycyrrhiza glabra and clean them thoroughly.
Grind 100g of the cleaned roots into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix 1000ml of pure vodka with the powdered root in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture at room temperature for six weeks, shaking the jar daily to macerate the ingredients.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and measuring the resulting tincture.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bell pepper tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it supports thyroid function by increasing iodine uptake, which is essential for hormone production. The antioxidants and vitamins in bell peppers also help to protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and inflammation, promoting a healthy metabolic rate. Additionally, bell pepper's unique combination of bioactive compounds may stimulate the release of thyroid hormones, regulating metabolism and energy levels. This natural supplement can be especially beneficial for those experiencing hypothyroidism symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin.
remedy_properties: Ashwagandha: Helps regulate thyroid function by reducing stress and anxiety, which can exacerbate hypothyroidism symptoms. Ashwagandha's adaptogenic properties help balance the body's response to stress, promoting a healthy thyroid gland.

Bladderwrack: Contains iodine, an essential nutrient for thyroid function, which helps maintain healthy thyroid hormone production. Bladderwrack also has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help alleviate hypothyroidism symptoms.

Lycium barbarum (Goji Berry): Rich in antioxidants and adaptogenic compounds, Goji Berry may help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, both of which can contribute to hypothyroidism. Its compounds may also help regulate thyroid hormone production and metabolism.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the bell pepper plant contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland associated with hypothyroidism.

Seeds: Bell pepper seeds contain a rich source of omega-3 fatty acids, specifically GLA (gamma-linolenic acid), which has been shown to regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried capsicum annuum peppers in large quantities for processing.
Chop the dried peppers into small pieces and mix with 80-proof vodka at a ratio of 1:2.
Allow the mixture to macerate for four weeks, shaking the container daily, to extract bioactive compounds.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into dark glass bottles, discarding solids.
Store the final tincture in a cool dark place for at least six months before using to ensure optimal potency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they contain a unique combination of vitamins, minerals, and antioxidants that support thyroid function. The tannins present in stinging nettle have been shown to increase the production of thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3), which are essential hormones produced by the thyroid gland. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of stinging nettle may help alleviate symptoms such as fatigue, dry skin, and joint pain commonly associated with hypothyroidism, promoting overall health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce thyroid inflammation and swelling, which can alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Immune system modulation: Nettle's immune-modulating effects may help regulate the immune response and reduce chronic inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism.

Thyroid hormone precursor synthesis support: Nettle contains compounds that may support the body's natural production of thyroid hormones by stimulating the conversion of inactive thyroxine (T4) into active triiodothyronine (T3), helping to balance thyroid function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the stinging nettle plant are rich in adaptogenic compounds, which help to regulate thyroid function and promote overall hormonal balance.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the stinging nettle plant contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds that have been shown to improve thyroid hormone production and metabolism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh nettle leaves and stems from a pesticide-free area in the morning after dew has evaporated.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator or oven on low heat for 24 hours.
Chop the dried nettle into small pieces and pack it tightly into clean glass jars leaving about an inch of headspace.
Combine 1 part chopped nettle with 2 parts high-proof vodka in each jar and seal them tightly storing them in a cool dark place for six weeks.
After six weeks strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container discarding the solids and transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because they support the liver's ability to detoxify and process hormones, which can become imbalanced in individuals with hypothyroidism. The tincture's diuretic properties also help to stimulate the thyroid gland, increasing its function and promoting the production of thyroxine (T4). Additionally, dandelion's antioxidant properties may help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, common underlying causes of hypothyroidism. By addressing these underlying factors, herbal dandelion tinctures can help to alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Dandelion tincture's antioxidant properties help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction and improve overall thyroid health.

Adrenal support: Dandelion root contains compounds that support adrenal function, which is closely tied to thyroid function, helping to alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as fatigue, weight gain, and depression.

Blood purification: Dandelion's bitter compounds help stimulate digestion and increase the body's natural elimination of toxins, including heavy metals that can interfere with thyroid hormone production.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and minerals, dandelion roots help to support thyroid function and reduce inflammation associated with hypothyroidism.
Leaves: High in vitamins A, C, and K, as well as calcium and iron, dandelion leaves provide essential nutrients that support overall health and hormone regulation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion roots at dawn on a sunny day for optimal potency and quality.
Rinse the harvested roots thoroughly with filtered water to remove any impurities and debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and macerate them in 80 proof vodka for exactly 14 days, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and reserving the liquid extract.
Label and store the tincture in a cool, dark place at room temperature for up to six months before use as directed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it contains compounds that have a profound impact on thyroid function. The tannins and flavonoids present in the extract have been shown to stimulate the production of thyroid hormones, effectively addressing symptoms of fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin often associated with hypothyroidism. remedy_properties: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties: Ginkgo biloba's antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the thyroid gland, which can alleviate hypothyroid symptoms.

Vasodilatory Effects: The tannins and flavonoids present in Maidenhair Tree tinctures increase blood flow to the thyroid gland, improving its function and potentially reducing fatigue and sluggishness associated with hypothyroidism.

Adaptogenic Properties: Ginkgo biloba's adaptogenic compounds help regulate the body's response to stress, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. By reducing cortisol levels and promoting a sense of calm, Maidenhair Tree tinctures may support thyroid function.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the maidenhair tree contain compounds that stimulate thyroid function, making them useful in treating hypothyroidism.
Barks: The barks of the maidenhair tree have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate symptoms associated with hypothyroidism, such as joint pain and swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ginkgo biloba leaves in the fall season when they reach their optimal potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using a food dehydrator at 95°f for 6 hours or by air drying them for 7 days.
Crush the dried ginkgo biloba leaves into small pieces using a mortar and pestle to increase their surface area.
Combine one part of the crushed ginkgo biloba with two parts of 80-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, shaking well to ensure saturation after 24 hours.
Steep the mixture for 6 weeks at room temperature, shaking the jar twice daily, before filtering and bottling the resulting tincture containing 1:4 ginkgo biloba extract in 25% ethanol by volume.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because of its unique ability to stimulate the production of thyroid hormones. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to increase thyroxine (T4) and triiodothyronine (T3) levels, alleviating symptoms such as fatigue, weight gain, and dry skin associated with hypothyroidism. Additionally, curcumin's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the thyroid gland, promoting a healthy environment for proper hormone production. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as curcumin, can reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to improved thyroid function and reduced symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Antioxidant property: Turmeric's antioxidants can help protect the thyroid gland from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to thyroid dysfunction and improve overall thyroid health.

Immunomodulatory property: Turmeric's immunomodulatory compounds can help regulate the immune system and reduce autoimmune responses that may be contributing to hypothyroidism, allowing for improved thyroid function and reduced symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in curcuminoids, including curcumin, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
Rhyzomes: As they are responsible for the production of volatile oils, rhyzomes provide a concentrated source of turmeric's active compounds, making them ideal for tincture production.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh turmeric rhizomes carefully selecting healthy roots without signs of disease or pests.
Wash the harvested turmeric rhizomes thoroughly under running water removing any dirt or debris.
Chop the washed turmeric rhizomes into small pieces and dry them in a dehydrator at 40°c for 12 hours.
Combine the dried turmeric with vodka in a ratio of 1:5 and store in an amber glass bottle steeping for 2 weeks.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another amber glass bottle discarding the solids and labeling accordingly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fenugreek tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it contains a unique set of compounds that have been shown to stimulate thyroid hormone production and improve overall thyroid function. Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in fenugreek tincture help protect the thyroid gland from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to hypothyroidism development or progression.

Hormone regulator property: Fenugreek tincture has been found to regulate hormone production and metabolism, including thyroid hormones, helping to restore balance and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in saponins and mucilages, fenugreek roots are known to stimulate thyroid function and improve metabolism.
Seeds: Fenugreek seeds are a rich source of galactomannans, which help to nourish and support the thyroid gland.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality trigonella foenum graecum leaves and stems, ensuring they are free from contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or shade drying for 7-10 days to prevent mold growth and preserve potency.
Combine 250 grams of dried trigonella foenum graecum material with 1250 milliliters of high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring the plant material is fully submerged.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar twice daily to enhance extraction and prevent settling.
Strain the tincture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids and storing the liquid in a dark glass bottle at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because its bioactive compounds, flavonoids and polyphenols, have been shown to enhance thyroid function by increasing the production of triiodothyronine (T3) and thyroxine (T4). remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried crataegus monogyna leaves and stems in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Chop the harvested plant material into small pieces and pack it into a clean glass jar leaving about an inch of space at the top.
Cover the chopped plant material with 25-30% vodka by volume and seal the jar tightly to prevent contamination.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6-8 weeks shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction and allow solvents to dissolve active compounds.

After the preparation time strain the tincture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass bottle discarding the solids and label the tincture with date dosage instructions and any relevant warnings.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Red clover tinctures helps with hypothyroidism because it contains isoflavones, which have been shown to stimulate thyroid function and increase the production of thyroid hormones. The isoflavones in red clover also help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the thyroid gland, which can contribute to hypothyroidism. Additionally, red clover has been found to increase the sensitivity of the thyroid gland to thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH), allowing for more effective regulation of thyroid function. This natural approach can help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism, such as fatigue and weight gain, and promote overall thyroid health.
remedy_properties: Isoflavones: Red clover contains isoflavones, which have been shown to support thyroid function by increasing T4 levels and improving overall thyroid hormone balance.

Saponins: The saponin content in red clover tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with hypothyroidism, promoting a healthy immune response.

Phytoestrogens: Red clover's phytoestrogenic compounds, specifically isoflavones, may help alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism such as hot flashes, mood changes, and insomnia by supporting hormonal balance and reducing estrogen dominance.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of red clover contain a high concentration of isoflavones, which have been shown to help regulate thyroid function and alleviate symptoms of hypothyroidism.

Seeds: Red clover seeds are rich in omega-3 fatty acids, particularly GLA (gamma-linolenic acid), which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation associated with hypothyroidism.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality red clover flowers in full bloom during the summer solstice morning for maximum potency.
Clean and dry the harvested flowers thoroughly within 24 hours of collection using paper towels or clean cloth.
Combine one part trifolium pratense flowers with two parts vodka at room temperature and allow it to steep for 6-8 weeks.
Strain the tincture mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a dark glass bottle, discarding solids and preserving liquid.
Store the final red clover tincture in a cool dark place for up to 3 years at a dosage of 20-30 drops three times daily.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to hypothyroidism that are treated with herbal tinctures are adrenal fatigue, menopause symptoms, and chronic fatigue syndrome. Herbal tinctures such as ashwagandha, maca, and black cohosh are often used to address these conditions. These tinctures can help regulate hormonal imbalances, reduce inflammation, and promote energy and vitality. Additionally, herbs like ginseng and rhodiola rosea may also be used to support thyroid function and overall well-being.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat hypthyroidism with herbal tinctures is to combine a blend of Ashwagandha, Bladderwrack, and Guggul. Ashwagandha helps regulate the thyroid gland's function, while Bladderwrack provides iodine-rich nutrients essential for thyroid health. Guggul stimulates metabolism and energy production. Take 20-30 drops of the blend three times a day with water or add to food. Monitor your progress and adjust dosages as needed under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
